what_it_does: Implements the Bench room, a seven-rung client-side instrument that grows a multi-page artifact root-first. It reads live DNA faces (sequence, voice, layouts, grammar), derives a page plan and archetype sequence, lets the user set a brief, root, and titles, cycles through layout forms, renders SVG wireframes and a horizontal mini-deck, persists state in localStorage, and exports a spec via POST or clipboard fallback.
observations: Seven rungs: Brief, Root, Plan, Titles, Frames, Deck, Keep.
Reads four live faces: sequence, voice, layouts, grammar.
Derives PLAN from the sequence face using a client-side port of plan(arc, n).
Persists the growing spec in localStorage under the key 'dna-bench-spec'.
Generates hand-coded SVG wireframes for layout archetypes using grammar proportions and layout zones.
Renders a scrollable horizontal deck of mini paper-world page cards.
Checks voice invariants: banned hedge words, title length, and trailing periods.
Uses CSS variables, color-mix, and injected CSS rather than external stylesheets.
Keeps the one gold action locked until a purpose is recorded.
Falls back to clipboard copy when the /api/grown endpoint is unavailable.
standouts: Live derivation of the page plan and dial envelopes straight from the sequence face.
A wireframe SVG generator that maps layout archetypes and grammar proportions into per-zone graphics.
Root-first generation ladder where each rung is treated as a complete artifact-so-far.
Deck cards that choose organism visuals based on the lead narrative role.
The Keep action as the single gold export moment with offline clipboard fallback.
conventions: IIFE with var-declared helper functions and mutable module-level state.
DOM building through concatenated HTML strings and innerHTML injection.
CSS injected once as a dynamically created <style> element.
Event wiring via data-* attributes and delegated querySelector loops.
State saved to and restored from localStorage as plain JSON.
Promise.all face loading with a fetch fallback for each dna/*.json file.
concerns: Large monolithic file mixing state, plan logic, rendering, event wiring, and CSS.
Heavy use of innerHTML with manual escaping; a missed escape could introduce XSS.
Many magic numbers and coordinate constants in SVG wireframe generation.
CSS is appended to document.head on every mount with no cleanup or deduplication.
Timers and event listeners are not explicitly torn down on room leave.
localStorage parsing has no schema validation or migration.
The /api/grown endpoint and CTX.org helpers are assumed but not defined in this file.
notes: A self-contained, face-driven generative workbench that turns a root line into a planned, titled, framed, and previewed artifact.
questions: What is the exact schema of the sequence, voice, layouts, and grammar faces?
What do CTX.org.hubNetwork, CTX.org.mesh, CTX.org.cascade, and CTX.org.detailStrip return?
What is the signature and behavior of window.DNA.ordinal and CTX.tok()?
How is the room unmounted, and are the injected style and timers cleaned up?
What does the server expect at /api/grown?
What is the intended relationship between the Bench and other rooms in the house?
How are face versions and token contexts managed across reloads?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file is explicitly a root-first, live-face-derived generation ladder with seven rungs and a keep action.
apparent_themes: Live derivation from shared DNA faces
Warmth, register, and density as narrative dials
intention_signals: It tries to make artifact generation feel like tuning an instrument: every rung is a complete valid artifact-so-far, all values flow from shared DNA faces, and constraints guide rather than block creation.
novelty: The tight coupling of narrative role planning, layout-archetype cycling, hand-coded SVG wireframe generation, and a scrollable mini-deck preview inside one browser room.
contribution: A stateful, interactive generator UI that consumes live DNA faces and produces a structured, exportable artifact spec with plan, titles, frames, and deck preview, including persistence and offline fallback.
summary_for_embedding: The Bench room is a client-side, seven-rung artifact generator that derives page plans, layout archetypes, wireframes, and a mini deck from live DNA faces and persists the growing spec in localStorage.
intention_for_embedding: To give users a root-first instrument for growing a complete piece from a single line, with every rung derived from shared design faces and every page previewed before it is kept.

symbols.does: Returns a fresh default spec object.
symbols.description: Creates the initial Bench state with step 1, a default brief, and empty root, titles, and frames arrays.

symbols.does: Restores the spec from localStorage if present.
symbols.description: Parses the JSON stored under 'dna-bench-spec' and merges it into the default blank state, silently ignoring errors.

symbols.does: Writes the current spec to localStorage.
symbols.description: Serializes the global S object to JSON and stores it under 'dna-bench-spec', silently ignoring storage errors.

symbols.does: Shorthand for document.getElementById.
symbols.description: A minimal DOM selector helper used throughout the Bench to fetch elements by id.

symbols.does: Escapes regex metacharacters in a string.
symbols.description: Prefixes special characters with backslashes so a literal string can be safely embedded in a regular expression.

symbols.does: Clamps a number between a lower and upper bound.
symbols.description: Utility used to keep page counts and other numeric values within allowed ranges.

symbols.does: Strips parenthetical suffixes from archetype names.
symbols.description: Turns strings like 'split-bleed (timeline)' into 'split-bleed' for affinity matching.

symbols.does: Returns an ordinal color from the live DNA ramp.
symbols.description: Calls window.DNA.ordinal with the current token context to pick a color for plan chips and bands.

symbols.does: Scales a fixed-size SVG organism into a smaller slot.
symbols.description: Wraps an SVG in a scaled-down container so it can be reused at different sizes, such as in deck cards.

symbols.does: Debounces deck re-rendering.
symbols.description: Clears any pending deck render timer and schedules a new render after 220 ms to avoid excessive updates during typing.

symbols.does: Looks up a voice invariant message by id.
symbols.description: Searches the live voice face's invariants array and returns the matching message text, or an empty string if not found.

symbols.does: Checks text against voice invariants.
symbols.description: Detects banned hedge words, title length violations, and trailing periods, returning an array of warning messages.

symbols.does: Renders voice notes under an input and toggles warning styling.
symbols.description: Displays invariant warnings in a note element and adds a warning class to the input when notes exist.

symbols.does: Computes a page's dial envelope from narrative roles.
symbols.description: Aggregates warmth ranges and averages, plus register and density, from the sequence face's narrative_roles for a given role group.

symbols.does: Computes the min/max page count for an arc.
symbols.description: Sums the count ranges of each run in the named arc from the sequence face.

symbols.does: Computes the allowed page range for the current brief.
symbols.description: Intersects the selected tier's page range with the selected arc's page range.

symbols.does: Builds the page plan from the live sequence face.
symbols.description: Produces PLAN entries with roles, archetype affinities, chosen archetype, and dial envelope, handling both fusion tiers and full-arc expansion.

symbols.does: Trims or pads titles and frames to match the plan length.
symbols.description: Ensures S.titles and S.frames have exactly one entry per planned page, preserving existing values when possible.

symbols.does: Returns the selected archetype for a plan page.
symbols.description: Uses the page's affinity list and the user's frame cycle offset to determine which archetype is active.

symbols.does: Collapses the plan into contiguous role runs.
symbols.description: Groups adjacent pages that share the same role set, recording start index and length.

symbols.does: Returns a page's position within its role run.
symbols.description: Counts backward from the given page index until the role set changes.

symbols.does: Determines whether a rung is accessible.
symbols.description: Rungs 1 are always open; rung 2 needs a subject; later rungs need both subject and root.

symbols.does: Returns a human-readable page-count label for a tier.
symbols.description: Describes tagline, fusion, or full tiers using live sequence data, including arc-derived ranges.

symbols.does: Counts how many titles are non-empty.
symbols.description: Filters S.titles for trimmed non-empty strings and returns the count.

symbols.does: Computes the summary text shown in a rung header.
symbols.description: Produces a short status string for each rung based on current brief, plan, titles, frames, and deck state.

symbols.does: Reads grammar proportions for an archetype.
symbols.description: Fetches the 'parts' array from the grammar face's proportion.archetypes entry, falling back to a provided default.

symbols.does: Builds an SVG rect string.
symbols.description: A helper that emits a rounded rectangle with common stroke and opacity defaults for wireframes.

symbols.does: Draws a small ochre corner marker.
symbols.description: Returns an SVG path representing the one-focal indicator used in wireframe previews.

symbols.does: Draws a hatched footer rectangle.
symbols.description: Returns an SVG group with a nested fill rectangle and diagonal lines for footer bands.

symbols.does: Draws title-bar placeholders.
symbols.description: Emits two rectangles representing a small label and a larger title in wireframe title zones.

symbols.does: Draws a series of decaying horizontal bars.
symbols.description: Used for text-line placeholders in wireframes, with widths decreasing per stripe.

symbols.does: Generates a wireframe SVG for a layout archetype.
symbols.description: Composes rectangles, focal markers, and zone-specific graphics for immersive, split-bleed, stack, field, duo, grid, trio, and title-main-footer layouts based on the live layouts and grammar faces.

symbols.does: Places a focal marker when a zone key matches the layout focal.
symbols.description: Inner helper of wireSVG that renders the ochre corner marker only on the designated focal zone.

symbols.does: Mixes warm and cool colors by warmth midpoint.
symbols.description: Returns a CSS color-mix expression used as the background tint for deck cards.

symbols.does: Builds rotating statistic couplets for deck cards.
symbols.description: Returns an array of [value, label] pairs rotated by page index for visual variety.

symbols.does: Formats statistic couplets as HTML.
symbols.description: Renders up to k statistic pairs, optionally enlarging the value text.

symbols.does: Returns example bullet claim fragments.
symbols.description: Pulls from the voice face's bullet_claim examples to show placeholder content in deck cards.

symbols.does: Derives hub-network node labels from the subject.
symbols.description: Uses the first letters of subject words, or falls back to canonical arc role initials, for the 'show' role org diagram.

symbols.does: Picks an organism visualization for a page's lead role.
symbols.description: Returns a hub network, mesh, couplets, cascade, or detail strip depending on whether the lead role is show, argue, prove, plan, or people.

symbols.does: Generates placeholder text-line divs.
symbols.description: Emits a series of divs with decreasing widths to stand in for body text in deck cards.

symbols.does: Renders an immersive one-pager deck card.
symbols.description: Used for open/close immersive pages, showing the root line, warmth/register, and a house icon.

symbols.does: Renders a single mini deck page card.
symbols.description: Chooses layout-specific inner content based on the page archetype and lead role, including title, organisms, couplets, bands, or plates.

symbols.does: Renders the horizontal deck strip.
symbols.description: Builds all page cards and injects them into the bn-deckstrip container, or a single book card when no plan exists.

symbols.does: Builds a selectable chip button HTML string.
symbols.description: Creates a button with label, optional sub-label, and an 'on' class for the currently selected value.

symbols.does: Builds the page-count picker HTML.
symbols.description: Generates chip buttons for every allowed page count within the current arc range.

symbols.does: Renders the Brief rung UI.
symbols.description: Builds inputs and chips for subject, reader, occasion, purpose, tier, page count, and arc, plus notes and the next button.

symbols.does: Renders the Root rung UI.
symbols.description: Builds the big root-line input, voice-note area, generation-ladder guidance, and next button.

symbols.does: Renders the Plan rung UI.
symbols.description: Displays the derived plan as a ribbon of role chips with warmth/register/density, or a message when the root is the whole piece.

symbols.does: Builds the title row preview HTML.
symbols.description: Shows either the root line or a sequence of title segments separated by dots.

symbols.does: Renders the Titles rung UI.
symbols.description: Displays the title row preview and grouped title inputs per role run, each with live voice-note checking.

symbols.does: Builds a single wireframe card HTML.
symbols.description: Includes page number, role, SVG wireframe, archetype title/id, and a hint about cycling forms.

symbols.does: Renders the Frames rung UI.
symbols.description: Shows a grid of wireframe cards that can be clicked to cycle through a page's allowed archetypes.

symbols.does: Renders the Deck rung UI.
symbols.description: Contains the horizontal deck strip and the button to proceed to Keep.

symbols.does: Renders the Keep rung UI.
symbols.description: Shows a digest of the brief, plan, titles, and face versions, plus the Keep button and its lock state.

symbols.does: Builds the outer HTML for a ladder rung.
symbols.description: Creates the section, header button with marker/number/name/result, and collapsible body container.

symbols.does: Updates the result line in a rung header.
symbols.description: Toggles locked/done states and animates the result text when it changes.

symbols.does: Refreshes all rung result lines.
symbols.description: Calls setRes for rungs 1 through 7.

symbols.does: Updates next-button and gate messages.
symbols.description: Enables/disables the Set the root and See the plan buttons based on subject and root presence.

symbols.does: Locks or unlocks the Keep button.
symbols.description: Requires a non-empty purpose before the gold Keep action is available.

symbols.does: Opens the selected rung and persists the step.
symbols.description: Checks unlock state, updates S.step, saves, toggles open classes, and refreshes result lines.

symbols.does: Renders a rung's body content and wires it.
symbols.description: Generates HTML via BODY[k] and attaches event listeners through wireBody.

symbols.does: Re-renders rungs affected by plan changes.
symbols.description: Starting from rung 3 or later, rebuilds bodies, re-renders the deck, and refreshes gates/results.

symbols.does: Shows a transient 'the change flows down the ladder' note.
symbols.description: Animates the brief-run note for a few seconds after structural changes.

symbols.does: Attaches event listeners to a rung's body.
symbols.description: Wires next buttons, brief chips, inputs, title fields, frame cycling, and the Keep action.

symbols.does: Cycles a frame card to its next archetype.
symbols.description: Increments the frame offset for a page, rebuilds the card with a fade transition, and updates deck/results.

symbols.does: Binds click and keyboard events to a frame card.
symbols.description: Attaches cycle handlers so users can tap or press Enter/Space to change archetypes.

symbols.does: Assembles the exportable artifact spec.
symbols.description: Collects brief, root, derived plan with archetypes and titles, face versions, and derivation note into a JSON object.

symbols.does: Persists the grown piece to the house or clipboard.
symbols.description: POSTs the spec to /api/grown; on failure copies JSON to the clipboard or shows a bench-stays-local message.

symbols.does: Shows a temporary status message.
symbols.description: Displays a fixed-position toast for 4.6 seconds and removes it afterward.
